visuals.title: Inter-Process Communication Models
visuals.description: Visual comparison of Shared Memory and Message Passing IPC models showing how processes exchange data through each mechanism.

diagrams.title: IPC Model Selection Flow
steps: Decision: Are processes on the same machine?
If same machine and large data: Use Shared Memory (fast, direct access)
If same machine and small data: Either model works
If distributed/different machines: Use Message Passing
Shared Memory: Set up shared region -> Producer writes -> Consumer reads
Message Passing: Sender calls send(msg) -> Kernel transfers -> Receiver calls receive(msg)
diagrams.notes: Shared memory requires only one system call (setup) but needs programmer-managed synchronization. Message passing uses system calls for every exchange but synchronization is implicit.
